summary.what_happened: session-store began mass-evicting at 13:00; broker consumers lost sessions and rebalanced; worker lag grew from 13:15. The alerting service is the end of the chain, not its start.

incident_window.rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-18T13:26:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; deviated series not recovered -> ongoing

severity.explanation: notification delivery lags by minutes but delivery retries exist and no booking-path service is degraded; no error-rate or latency series is in the package to place this in a numeric band

timeline.description: evictions_per_sec first deviated >=2.0x from baseline (2.0 keys/s -> 420.0 keys/s)
WARN: evicting keys under memory pressure: maxmemory 4gb reached (allkeys-lru)
queue_depth_msgs first deviated >=2.0x from baseline (150.0 msgs -> 480.0 msgs)
WARN: consumer session lookups failing; redelivery queue growing
consumer_lag_seconds first deviated >=2.0x from baseline (4.0 s -> 22.0 s)
ERROR: cannot restore consumer session: key not found in session-store; rejoining group
ERROR: cannot restore consumer session: key not found in session-store; rejoining group
ERROR: rebalance storm: 14 group rejoins in 5 minutes; throughput collapsed
Alert fired: Consumer lag above 30s on notifications-worker

evidence.interpretation: deploys.json empty: nothing shipped in the change window; capacity/state cause more plausible than change-driven
eviction warning 13:01 -> broker session-lookup failures 13:07 -> worker rejoin errors 13:16: the log chain follows the topology
notifications-worker lag first deviated at 13:15 (22s vs 4s baseline)
message-broker queue depth first deviated at 13:05 (480 vs 150)
session-store evictions first deviated at 13:00 (420/s vs 2/s) - the EARLIEST deviation, two topology hops from the alerting service

runbook documents this exact failure mode: 'session-store eviction cascade' - worker lag is named as the symptom, the store the cause; scaling workers during a rebalance storm is explicitly warned against

hypotheses.title: session-store eviction cascade through broker to workers
hypotheses.statement: session-store evictions (13:00) broke broker consumer sessions (13:07) causing worker rebalance churn and lag (13:15) - the deviation order matches the topology chain and the runbook's documented cascade.
assumptions: package telemetry is complete for the window
recommended_checks: confirm session-store memory limit and what grew into it
verify consumer session TTLs against eviction age

missing_data.impact: cannot identify slow spans or failing dependencies

recommended_next_steps.description: confirm session-store memory limit and what grew into it
verify consumer session TTLs against eviction age
Provide or repair the package data: traces.json not provided

safe_mitigation_options.action: Raise session-store maxmemory one step per the runbook
risks: higher memory footprint on the cache host

remediation_plans.title: Consider raising session-store maxmemory one step
preconditions: runbook marks the step verified safe (keys re-derivable)

steps.action: confirm current maxmemory and eviction rate
steps.verification: store INFO shows the expected limit

steps.action: raise session-store maxmemory one step per runbook
steps.verification: evictions fall below 100 keys/s and broker redelivery stops growing within 10 minutes

abort_conditions: store memory saturates again within 10 minutes of the raise

signals.recovered_when: within 10% of baseline 150 msgs for >= 3 consecutive points
within 10% of baseline 4 s for >= 3 consecutive points
within 10% of baseline 2 keys/s for >= 3 consecutive points

log_patterns_should_stop: cannot restore consumer session: key not found in session-store; rejoining group
recovery_verification.re_alert_condition: consumer_lag_seconds on notifications-worker crosses the alert threshold 30 again

checks.detail: no numeric signals to check the claimed level against

communication_drafts.internal_update: SEV-3, ongoing: notifications delayed minutes. Leading hypothesis (high confidence): session-store eviction cascade - deviations follow the topology chain store->broker->worker and match the runbook's documented failure mode. Do NOT scale workers (runbook: worsens rebalance churn). No remediation has been executed; the maxmemory raise awaits human approval.

status_page.text: Some notifications are currently delayed by a few minutes. We have identified the cause and a fix is being prepared. No notifications are lost; delayed messages will be delivered.

postmortem_draft.title: Postmortem draft: notification lag 2026-06-18
postmortem_draft.summary: session-store memory pressure caused mass evictions; broker consumers lost sessions and worker throughput collapsed into rebalance churn.
postmortem_draft.impact: Notification delivery delayed up to 4 minutes.
contributing_factors: likely: session-store sized below current session volume
open_questions: what grew memory usage to the limit
action_items: alert on eviction rate; review store sizing
